name: Emma Chen
notes: VIP returning guest. Mentioned during last stay that she founded a women's cooperative in Vietnam. Do not offer any non-sustainable menu options without flagging sourcing.
briefingTalkingPoints: The chicken on tonight's menu comes from Traspatio — a women-led cooperative. Every order funds their livelihoods directly.
99% of this menu was sourced within 60 miles. The 1% is salt.
Pirules is working toward becoming the first plastic-free restaurant in Latin America — it's not a goal, it's a project in progress.

name: Dr. James Park
notes: Longevity researcher based in Seoul and San Francisco. Interested in bioavailability, omega sourcing, and minimal-processing techniques. Does not eat red meat.
briefingTalkingPoints: The king crab is certified by Smart Catch and Ocean Wise — both evaluate population health, not just catch method.
Chef Falsini grows the herbs in an on-property garden — 30 feet from the kitchen. The honey comes from a beehive on the same grounds.
We can speak to the omega-3 content of the seafood sourcing if he asks — the Ocean Wise certification requires this data.

name: Isabelle Moreau
notes: Food writer and anthropologist based in Paris. Has written extensively on Southeast Asian culinary traditions. Will ask detailed cultural context questions — be ready with the specific regional story, not just 'traditional Thai.'
briefingTalkingPoints: Massaman is not just a Thai dish — it entered Thailand from Muslim spice trade routes. The word Massaman derives from 'Musulman.' This is Chef Bua's version, refined over 30 years.
Chef Bua trained in royal court techniques — a distinct discipline from street or home cooking. Ask her anything.
The crispy durian is from Southern Thailand specifically — durian quality is highly regional, like wine appellations.

name: Robert Ashford
notes: Managing Director at a global PE firm. First visit. Does not appreciate lengthy explanations — lead with credentials and let him ask follow-up questions. Excellent tipping record. Prefers red wine.
briefingTalkingPoints: Caruso's is one of very few US restaurants with both a Michelin Star and a Michelin Green Star.
The crab was in the ocean within 50 miles of this table.
Leave space for him to ask questions — he will if he's interested.
